data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Alters Claude Subscription Rules, OpenClaw Responds
data.description: Anthropic has revised its subscription rules for Claude, affecting the inclusion of third-party tools like OpenClaw. According to Odaily, users now face additional charges for such integrations. In response, OpenClaw suggested on the X platform that users could switch to using an API Key to avoid billing complexities. Alternatively, they can consider other subscription options such as OpenAI Codex, Qwen, MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM.

data.title: Potential U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Boosts Market Sentiment, Bitcoin and Major Cryptos Rise
data.description:
Market sentiment has improved amid expectations of a potential ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, leading to increased risk appetite. According to Odaily, the ceasefire plan might take effect on Monday, potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This development has contributed to Bitcoin's rise, strengthening the overall crypto market, with major assets like Ethereum and Solana also experiencing gains. The derivatives market is signaling bullish trends, with improvements in Bitcoin futures structure and a continued decline in 30-day implied volatility. Additionally, Nasdaq 100 futures have risen by 0.8%, reinforcing the risk-on atmosphere.

However, analysts caution that the market rebound remains uncertain. Previous ceasefire announcements have been denied or disproven by Iran, and similar occurrences could lead to a swift market reversal. Furthermore, even if an agreement is reached, its binding effect on Israel remains questionable, potentially limiting the sustainability of risk appetite. Overall, while Bitcoin has upward potential, macroeconomic variables continue to be a significant constraint.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's rise boosted the overall crypto market, while expectations of a ceasefire in Iran drove risk-on trading.
data.description: Boosted by expectations of a potential ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, market risk appetite has rebounded. The ceasefire plan is reportedly set to take effect on Monday, potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Bitcoin's rise has driven the overall crypto market higher, with Ethereum, Solana, and other major assets also rising. The derivatives market has also released bullish signals, with Bitcoin futures showing improved structure and 30-day implied volatility continuing to decline. Meanwhile, Nasdaq 100 futures rose 0.8%, reinforcing the risk-on atmosphere. However, analysts point out that the market rebound still faces uncertainty. Recent ceasefire announcements have been repeatedly denied or refuted by Iran; if similar situations occur again, the market could quickly reverse. Furthermore, even if an agreement is reached, its binding force on Israel remains questionable, potentially limiting the sustainability of risk appetite. Overall, while Bitcoin has upside potential, macroeconomic variables remain a key constraint. (CoinDesk) Note: "Risk-on" is a common term in financial markets, meaning the market is in a "risk-averse" state, where investors are willing to take on more risk to pursue returns.

data.title: QCP: Crypto markets downplay escalating Iranian risks, but the sustainability of the latest rally remains to be seen.
data.description: A recent analysis by QCP Capital points out that US President Trump's decision to take action against Iran, postponed again until Tuesday for the fourth time, indicates that the market is gradually becoming immune to the recurring pattern of "tough statements + negotiating signals," cooling expectations of escalating risk. Oil prices weakened, while stock index futures remained stable. In the crypto market, Bitcoin and Ethereum broke through $69,000 and $2,140 respectively during Asian trading hours, triggering approximately $200 million in short liquidations in a illiquid environment. Overall, despite continued geopolitical disturbances, price performance leans more towards stabilization than downward pressure. In terms of funding, institutional funds continue to provide support, with the Bitcoin ETF recording approximately $1.32 billion in net inflows in March. The current market is generally risk-on, with investors not fully prepared for a short-term escalation of conflict. However, with the reopening of US stocks, the sustainability of this rebound remains to be seen.

data.title: OpenClaw responds to Anthropic's subscription rule adjustment: Other subscription services such as API Key or MiniMax can be used.
data.description: In response to Anthropic's adjustment of its Claude subscription rules, which no longer covers third-party tool calls, including OpenClaw, and requires additional payment for such usage paths, OpenClaw issued a statement on the X platform stating that to avoid billing complexity, users can switch to API Keys or consider other subscription options, including OpenAI Codex, Qwen, MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM.

data.title: On-chain perpetual contract trading cools down: DEX trading volume has declined for five consecutive months, shrinking by nearly 50% from its peak.
data.description: According to DefiLlama data, on-chain perpetual contract (Perp) trading has declined for five consecutive months since peaking in October 2025. In March 2026, Perp DEX trading volume dropped to $699 billion, a significant decline from the peak of $1.36 trillion in October of last year. Furthermore, daily data also shows weakening activity; on April 4th, daily trading volume fell to $8.4 billion, the first time it has fallen below $10 billion since September 2025, and a new low since July 2025, reflecting a continued cooling of demand for leveraged trading and speculation. In addition, current Perp DEX trading volume remains highly concentrated on top platforms. Over the past 30 days, Hyperliquid recorded approximately $185.5 billion in trading volume, accounting for about 34% of the total trading volume of the top ten platforms. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: HYPE Token Distribution Remains Concentrated
data.description: According to HyperTracker's latest data, the number of addresses holding HYPE tokens has not yet exceeded 250,000. According to Odaily, only 51,727 addresses hold more than 10 HYPE tokens, while high-net-worth wallets with over 1,000 tokens number just 7,177. This indicates that the token distribution remains relatively concentrated.

data.title: Iran Launches Missile Strikes in 'True Promise-4' Operation
data.description:
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has announced the launch of the 98th wave of its 'True Promise-4' operation. According to Odaily, the IRGC's naval forces targeted a container ship, SDN7, associated with the 'Zionist regime' using cruise missiles, resulting in a large fire after the ship was destroyed. Additionally, missile strikes were carried out on strategic centers in northern and southern Tel Aviv, Haifa, chemical enterprises and factories in Beersheba, and a military base in Bat Hefer.

The statement also mentioned that the U.S. military's amphibious assault ship, LHA7, carrying over 5,000 sailors and marines, was hit by missiles and subsequently forced to retreat to the deep southern Indian Ocean. Furthermore, a joint drone production center between the UAE and Israel, along with several aircraft stationed at Ali Al Salem base, were targeted by drones and missiles.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Near $70K as Trump-Iran Headlines Trigger $70B Crypto Rally and Short Liquidations
data.description: Key TakeawaysCrypto market cap rises $70B (+2.5%) as Donald Trump signals possible Iran deal.Bitcoin climbs toward $70,000, with over $250M–$270M in short liquidations.Oil remains elevated near $112, keeping inflation risks in focus.Derivatives show rising open interest, but options markets remain cautious.Crypto Markets Rally on Ceasefire HopesBitcoin and the broader crypto market moved higher after mixed signals from Donald Trump regarding a potential Iran deal and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.Total crypto market capitalization increased by approximately $70 billion, reaching $2.44 trillion, while Bitcoin climbed to around $69,500–$70,000.The rally coincided with reports of potential ceasefire talks, including a proposed 45-day truce, boosting short-term risk appetite across global markets.Short Liquidations Drive Price ActionThe move higher triggered significant liquidations in derivatives markets.Data shows more than $250 million to $270 million in liquidations, with around 70%+ coming from short positions, indicating a short squeeze contributed to the rally.This suggests the upward move was partly driven by forced position closures rather than purely new demand.Mixed Signals Keep Markets CautiousDespite bullish price action, markets remain sensitive to conflicting geopolitical signals.Trump issued strong warnings to Iran over reopening the Strait of Hormuz while simultaneously indicating negotiations are underway and a deal could be reached within days.This combination of escalation and de-escalation narratives has created a highly reactive, headline-driven market environment. Oil Prices Remain a Key Macro RiskCrude oil remains elevated near $112 per barrel, reflecting ongoing supply concerns.Sustained high energy prices could push inflation higher, with estimates suggesting U.S. CPI could rise toward 3.7% if current levels persist.Higher inflation may delay Federal Reserve rate cuts, potentially limiting upside for risk assets, including crypto.Derivatives Data Shows Mixed ConvictionFutures markets show signs of renewed participation.Bitcoin and Ethereum open interest increased by 7% and 11%, respectively, with positive funding rates indicating traders are opening long positions.However, options markets remain defensive. On Deribit, demand for put options continues to exceed calls, reflecting ongoing hedging activity.Key levels to watch include $60,000 (downside) and $80,000 (upside), where large options positions are concentrated.Altcoins Outperform, Led by AlgorandAltcoins outpaced Bitcoin during the rally.Algorand (ALGO) surged nearly 50% over the past month, supported by increased visibility following research from Google Quantum AI highlighting its quantum-resistant technology.Other altcoins also saw gains, reflecting improved market breadth during the rebound.Rally Depends on Geopolitical ResolutionThe crypto market remains highly dependent on geopolitical developments.If a ceasefire is confirmed and oil prices decline, risk assets could extend gains. However, continued uncertainty or escalation may reverse momentum.For now, the rally appears partly liquidation-driven and headline-sensitive, with sustained upside requiring clearer macro stabilization and stronger spot demand.

data.title: Israeli Defense Minister Orders Continued Strikes on Iran's Infrastructure
data.description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz has announced that he has instructed the military to persist with comprehensive strikes targeting Iran's national infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, Katz characterized the operation as an ongoing all-out campaign.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Resolves API Service Disruption
data.description:
On April 6, OpenAI announced a status update regarding the restoration of its /v1/responses API interface. According to BlockBeats, the service had experienced an outage earlier that day.

The disruption occurred around 18:07 UTC+8, affecting the functionality of the API. OpenAI initiated an investigation and reported at 18:23 that the issue was largely mitigated and entered a monitoring phase.

By 18:39, OpenAI confirmed that all affected components were fully restored, marking the resolution of the incident.

data.title: Israeli Defense Minister: I have instructed the Israeli military to continue its full-scale strikes against Iran's "national infrastructure."
data.description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz: I have instructed the Israeli military to continue its full-scale strikes against Iran's "national infrastructure." (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Suggests Ethereum Needs to Reclaim $2,500 for Bullish Reversal
data.description: According to NS3.AI, analyst Ali Martinez has indicated that Ethereum must surpass the $2,500 mark to confirm a bullish reversal. Martinez highlighted that the $1,800 level serves as a potentially strong support zone, being near the MVRV-based level of approximately $1,880.

data.title: Federal Charges Filed in $53.3 Million Uranium Finance Theft Case
data.description: Federal prosecutors have charged Jonathan Spalletta in connection with the alleged theft of approximately $53.3 million from Uranium Finance. According to NS3.AI, the charges also include allegations of laundering the stolen funds through Tornado Cash. The case originates from two smart contract exploits in 2021, one of which affected 26 liquidity pools and resulted in the shutdown of Uranium Finance. Spalletta could face a maximum sentence of 10 years for computer fraud and up to 20 years for money laundering.

data.title: Global Food Prices Increase Amid Rising Energy Costs
data.description: The World Food Price Index experienced a 2.4% increase in March, reaching 128.5 points, driven by surging energy costs. According to NS3.AI, the rise in freight costs and disruptions in the flow of grains and fertilizers through the Strait of Hormuz were significant contributors to this increase. The index monitors global prices for grains, sugar, meat, dairy, and vegetable oils. Meanwhile, discussions are underway between the US, Iran, and regional mediators regarding a potential 45-day ceasefire, which could pave the way for a permanent resolution to the ongoing conflict.

data.title: BlackRock transferred a total of approximately 1,178 BTC, worth about $82.25 million, to a Coinbase Prime address.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring data, about 32 minutes ago, BlackRock transferred a total of about 1,178 BTC, worth about $82.25 million, to Coinbase Prime via IBIT, and there may be further transfers in the future.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Recovers Lost BTC with imBack's Assistance
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Jack Yi announced on social media that imBack, a crypto asset recovery service, successfully unlocked an iPhone that had been sealed for 18 years, retrieving BTC previously thought to be permanently lost. According to ChainCatcher, Yi had lost hope of recovering the wallet, but imBack's technical capabilities exceeded his expectations. Following this experience, Yi quickly invested in imBack. The company reportedly offers recovery services for early users who hold BTC, ETH, and other assets but cannot access their wallets due to device lockouts or lost private keys.

data.title: China Encourages Blockchain Use in Lending to Boost Small Business Financing
data.description: China's tax and financial regulators have called on banks and local authorities to integrate blockchain and privacy computing technologies into lending services. According to NS3.AI, this initiative is part of China's broader strategy to invest 400 billion yuan annually in blockchain data infrastructure. The joint policy notice emphasizes the need for standardized data sharing to minimize information asymmetry and enhance financing opportunities for small businesses.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 10 million ASTER tokens from Binance, worth $6.7 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet withdrew 10 million ASTER tokens from Binance, worth $6.7 million.
